paragraphs.text: Paramount A SKYDANCE CORPORATION

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act

paragraphs.text: Paramount has a strong European history, dating back to 1915, when we produced our first film in Europe – The Eternal City, a silent drama set in Rome. Our studios have continued to shoot and produce films and TV content in Europe ever since, partnering with the vibrant European cultural and creative industry and contributing to its vitality and global success – the final two instalments of the Mission: Impossible franchise, Gladiator II and Emily in Paris were all filmed in Europe, just to name a few. Our linear TV channels, such as MTV, Nickelodeon and Comedy Central, have been household names across the continent since the 1980s, both on free-to-air and pay-tv platforms.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
About Paramount

evidence_quotes.quote: digital subscription video on-demand (SVOD)

paragraphs.text: Our commitment to Europe has remained steadfast in the streaming era. Paramount+, our digital subscription video on-demand (SVOD) and live streaming service, is available to consumers in 5 EU Member States (Germany, France, Italy, Austria and Ireland). Our content is available in many other EU Member States on SkyShowtime, the SVOD service we operate in joint-venture with Comcast. We also operate Pluto TV, the leading free ad-supported streaming television (FAST) service delivering hundreds of live linear channels and thousands of titles on-demand to EU audiences.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
About Paramount

paragraphs.text: European consumers are important to us in so many ways – as movie-goers, TV-watchers, streaming subscribers, and fans. We rely on them to generate revenues that we can reinvest in bringing new stories and entertainment experiences to life for European and global audiences – hence contributing to the competitiveness and sustainability of the European audiovisual industry. That is why we strive to constructively engage with and fully respect all consumers, both online and offline.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: Therefore, we welcome the opportunity to offer our views on the Digital Fairness Act consultation and the fact that the European Commission intends to keep ensuring a high-level of consumer protection and introduce simplification measures for businesses to enhance competitiveness.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: We believe that the existing EU legislative framework ensures a sufficient level of protection for all consumers across all their interactions with streaming platforms. Moreover, the current framework provides the necessary legal clarity and enough flexibility for responsible traders like us to be commercially successful while protecting consumer rights. Introducing new regulatory or non-regulatory measures applying to regulated businesses like ours in the entertainment sector would not only run counter to the key goals of simplification and competitiveness, but it is not warranted by actual risks of consumer harm.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: As it considers the best way forward to achieve its key objectives of protecting consumers and reducing the regulatory burden, we encourage the European Commission to be flexible and proportionate in its approach by adopting the following principles:

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: Focus on actual risks, no one-size-fits-all: Assess the different risk levels for consumers generated by practices typical of different business models and adopt a risk-based approach that is proportionate and caters for sectoral specificities.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: Simplification, not duplication: Take into account existing sectoral legislation – such as the AVMSD – and self-regulatory initiatives that already endow EU citizens with additional protections and avoid duplicating existing rules or overburdening already regulated sectors.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

evidence_quotes.quote: the economic viability and competitiveness of specific industries, such as the media sector,

paragraphs.text: Enable competitiveness: Carefully consider the potential direct and indirect impacts of any new overly prescriptive measures both on the economic viability and competitiveness of specific industries, such as the media sector, as well as on businesses' ability to best serve consumers.

section_path: Response to the European Commission consultation on the Digital Fairness Act
Why we care about EU consumers and our suggested approach on digital fairness

paragraphs.text: This chapter responds to Sections 6, 7, and 8 of the consultation.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

paragraphs.text: Streaming subscribers are familiar and continuously connected with the providers of digital content they purchase, as well as their rights and contractual circumstances. Intensive competition obliges providers to maintain goodwill through transparent and respectful relationships. No further regulation is required. The Commission should therefore:

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: allowing consumers to waive their right of withdrawal from all contracts for digital content

paragraphs.text: Protect streaming business models and investment by allowing consumers to waive their right of withdrawal from all contracts for digital content, so avoiding ‘binge and refund’ habits and preserving settled industry practice.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: active ‘opt-in’ requirements for subscribers at the end of free trials or contract renewals.

paragraphs.text: Avoid additional burden on consumers and undesired cancellations through active ‘opt-in’ requirements for subscribers at the end of free trials or contract renewals.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: partial repayments for cancelled subscriptions

paragraphs.text: Avoid a ‘compromise solution’ of partial repayments for cancelled subscriptions, which would undermine business models and be complex and burdensome to implement.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: Allow freedom and flexibility for streamers to decide when and how to communicate with consumers, avoiding reduced attention and connectivity through information overload.

paragraphs.text: Allow freedom and flexibility for streamers to decide when and how to communicate with consumers, avoiding reduced attention and connectivity through information overload.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: Permit price discounts and other offers to enable providers of streaming services to retain and increase their subscriber base.

paragraphs.text: Permit price discounts and other offers to enable providers of streaming services to retain and increase their subscriber base.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: video-on-demand subscription contracts
they subscribe to 2.62 streaming services
switching between AV subscription services

paragraphs.text: EU consumers are well acquainted with video-on-demand subscription contracts as clearly shown by data in the European Media Industry Outlook 2025. On average, they subscribe to 2.62 streaming services (both national and international). Moreover, consumers are cost-savvy and increasingly comfortable switching between AV subscription services, citing attractive price or promotions as the second most important factor in their decision on whether to subscribe to a new video streaming service. The most influential determinant for consumer preferences is content. The wide selection of

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

paragraphs.text: genres, value for money, the selection of films and series, and the availability of exclusive content are particularly well-rated by consumers when thinking about their preferred streaming service.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: Paramount+ is built on a subscription-based model.

paragraphs.text: Paramount+ is built on a subscription-based model. Consumers currently have the choice of subscribing for one month or one year at a time and, from the point of subscription, have access to the full Paramount+ library of content to enjoy. SVOD services like Paramount+ are a crucial part of the wider ecology feeding the screen sector. Revenue from subscriptions is reinvested in the production of new content and improvements of the service's functionalities, including content discoverability, allowing us to further extend the offer for consumers and improve their experience.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS

evidence_quotes.quote: sector-specific carveout on the right of withdrawal.
Allowing consumers the option to fully waive their right of withdrawal at the sign-up stage
a mandatory 14-day withdrawal period (with pro-rated payment)

paragraphs.text: We support a simplification proposal featuring a sector-specific carveout on the right of withdrawal. Allowing consumers the option to fully waive their right of withdrawal at the sign-up stage, in exchange for accessing digital content, is the only way to prevent a materially significant loss in revenue and with it the associated reduction in content investment. Without it, a mandatory 14-day withdrawal period (with pro-rated payment) would expose SVOD providers to practices such as 'binge and refund', 'cherry-picking' around high-profile releases, and serial account creation.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: including digital subscriptions.

paragraphs.text: The immediate and non-returnable nature of streamed digital content, once accessed, is already recognized by the recently amended Consumer Rights Directive (CRD), and this should continue to be the case. The CRD provides crucial pre-contractual information requirements and withdrawal rights specifically designed to protect consumers in distance contracts, including digital subscriptions. On transactional VOD services, consumers can waive their right of withdrawal by giving their informed consent to begin immediate supply of that content during the withdrawal period. This is a clear, balanced framework that consumers understand, and providers can operationalize.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: Partial refund obligations also raise additional challenges.
Secondly, consumers can subscribe to SVOD services through different types of contracts and user journeys (for example, through app stores and bundles).

paragraphs.text: Partial refund obligations also raise additional challenges. First, there is no obvious mechanism for calculating refunds that could be automated, and every refund carries an additional charge-back fee to the business. The amount of time a consumer has access to a streaming service does not necessarily reflect the value of the content consumed, for example if they have subscribed specifically to watch a high-profile new tentpole release or series. Equally, it would be unrealistic and burdensome to put a price tag on individual titles watched when calculating a refund. For example, the rights to titles regularly change from one AV player to the other, and it would be difficult to assess the value of older movies with respect to new additions. Secondly, consumers can subscribe to SVOD services through different types of contracts and user journeys (for example, through app stores and bundles). This means that SVOD providers are not always in a direct billing relationship with the consumer, making refund obligations even more disproportionately burdensome for SVOD providers to perform and incredibly difficult when the refund is controlled by the billing partner or distribution affiliate.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: right to cancel at the end of the first month
with or without pro-rated repayments

paragraphs.text: When considering alternative policy options, we encourage the Commission to keep in mind potential impacts on commercial viability of SVOD services and the sustainability of the AV sector more broadly. This is why we oppose the possible introduction of a right to cancel at the end of the first month, with or without pro-rated repayments, and regardless of the formal duration of the contract.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: cancellation right after the first month

paragraphs.text: It is also important to note that the average monthly cost of an annual subscription is typically cheaper than the sum of payments over a year for monthly subscriptions. The introduction of a cancellation right after the first month would therefore undermine streamers' incentives to offer annual subscription options, given that it would effectively give consumers the option of a cheaper monthly subscription.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: we support reducing information requirements for repetitive transactions.
consumers can subscribe to SVOD services (e.g. through different devices, bundles)

paragraphs.text: Finally, in line with the simplification agenda, we support reducing information requirements for repetitive transactions. Given the variety of ways in which consumers can subscribe to SVOD services (e.g. through different devices, bundles), it is important to maintain a broad, principle-based approach to information requirements. Highly prescriptive information requirements can be difficult to implement and lead to consumer frustration.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.1. Right of withdrawal waiver is critical to VOD business model and simplification is welcome

evidence_quotes.quote: ‘free trials’ are an important way for us to reach new consumers, who appreciate the opportunity to explore new services without having to make payments upfront.
Automatic renewal of subscriptions is another convenient feature for Paramount+ subscribers, who retain flexibility to cancel anytime.
If they choose to cancel, access to the content will end at the end of the billing period (as per standard industry practice).

paragraphs.text: As a more recent entrant in the SVOD market, ‘free trials’ are an important way for us to reach new consumers, who appreciate the opportunity to explore new services without having to make payments upfront. Automatic renewal of subscriptions is another convenient feature for Paramount+ subscribers, who retain flexibility to cancel anytime. If they choose to cancel, access to the content will end at the end of the billing period (as per standard industry practice).

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.2. Free trials and automatic renewals of streaming subscriptions are valued by consumers

evidence_quotes.quote: conversion from free trial to paid subscription

paragraphs.text: We want our subscribers to fully enjoy their subscription and watch our great content. This is why we are in regular contact with them, informing them of the new additions to our content catalogue and making sure they do not forget they have subscribed to Paramount+. This is true for all subscribers, including those who first explored Paramount+ on a free trial – we are especially glad they keep watching our content after the conversion from free trial to paid subscription, showcasing that they have not simply forgotten about their new subscription and value our offer.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.2. Free trials and automatic renewals of streaming subscriptions are valued by consumers

evidence_quotes.quote: requiring consumers to proactively act in order for them to continue to enjoy their subscription benefits at the end of a free trial or at the beginning of every new billing period – i.e. up to once per month – would likely encourage unnecessary subscriber churn

paragraphs.text: New prescriptive rules such as mandatory reminder notices or ‘opt-ins’ are unnecessary in the SVOD context, they would have a negative impact and pose serious practical challenges. First, prescriptive obligations on reminder notices or ‘opt-ins’ risk overwhelming consumers – e.g. with email fatigue – making it more difficult for us to engage them to make sure they are aware of the new content they might like or other important updates. Second, requiring consumers to proactively act in order for them to continue to enjoy their subscription benefits at the end of a free trial or at the beginning of every new billing period – i.e. up to once per month – would likely encourage unnecessary subscriber churn, with negative consequences for future investment. Finally, SVOD subscriptions often involve multiple parties (e.g. bundling offers, or via app stores), where SVOD providers like us have a limited margin of action with regards to these features. Therefore, any new obligations would bring additional administrative burdens and practical complexities, and responsibilities along the value chain would need to be clearly defined.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.2. Free trials and automatic renewals of streaming subscriptions are valued by consumers

evidence_quotes.quote: The cost of Paramount+ subscription plans is fully transparent for consumers visiting our website and apps.

paragraphs.text: We recognize the importance of full pricing transparency. The cost of Paramount+ subscription plans is fully transparent for consumers visiting our website and apps. We also sometimes offer special discounted prices to certain consumers, for example through partnerships with third parties. Therefore, we think that it is in consumers’ interest that any new rules should not undermine traders’ ability to make offers around pricing by making it disproportionately burdensome to do so.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.3. Enabling us to offer discounts is in the consumer interest

evidence_quotes.quote: subscription discounts and other cost-saving offers (e.g. bundling offers) are an important way to reach new consumers

paragraphs.text: The SVOD market is extremely competitive, and subscription discounts and other cost-saving offers (e.g. bundling offers) are an important way to reach new consumers, especially for newer market entrants. Therefore, a one-size-fits-all approach could lead to less cost-saving offers provided by SVOD services and would ultimately diminish consumer choice and harm media plurality.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
1.3. Enabling us to offer discounts is in the consumer interest

paragraphs.text: This chapter responds to Sections 1 and 2 of the public consultation.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

paragraphs.text: As licensed and well-regulated services, streaming platforms already have responsible and respectful relationships with their customers. The Commission should therefore balance its intervention proportionately to the level of risk of harm faced by consumers as follows:

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: cancellation button

paragraphs.text: Avoid prescriptive requirements for a ‘cancellation button’ on streaming platforms and permit them to make retention offers as part of any cancellation process.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: addictive design techniques identified in the consultation.

paragraphs.text: Recognize that ‘autoplay’ and content recommendation systems used by streamers are transparent, well-understood and valued by consumers, and are fundamentally different to addictive design techniques identified in the consultation.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

paragraphs.text: At Paramount, we put a lot of effort in ensuring that consumers enjoy the best possible experience when exploring the vast libraries of content available on our streaming services and watching their favorite films, shows and sports. We are regulated as a media service provider under the Audiovisual Media Services Directive (AVMSD). As such, the content we offer viewers is curated, professionally produced, and we have editorial responsibility over it. We take particular care in developing appealing and appropriate content for minors, through our historic brands, such as Nickelodeon.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: unfair manipulation
digital addiction

paragraphs.text: We work hard to provide content and features that consumers value. So, we are concerned about ideas associating certain consumer-friendly features on streaming services with serious issues like unfair manipulation and digital addiction.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: the most harmful practices that would fall in the category of so-called ‘dark patterns’ are already prohibited under EU law
including for profiling purposes

paragraphs.text: We understand that the Commission is concerned by unfair commercial practices that influence consumers to take decisions they would not have taken otherwise. Indeed, the most harmful practices that would fall in the category of so-called ‘dark patterns’ are already prohibited under EU law, across a variety of legislative instruments, including the Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (UCPD), Digital Services Act (DSA), Digital Markets Act (DMA) and Artificial Intelligence Act (AIA). In addition, the 2021 Commission Guidance on the UCPD is an effective tool for national consumer authorities to carry out enforcement actions on this matter without legal uncertainty. Finally, the restrictions on personal data processing established under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), including for profiling purposes, already help protect vulnerable consumers more susceptible to digital addiction.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: setting prescriptive rules on how all businesses design their online interfaces

paragraphs.text: We do not believe that setting prescriptive rules on how all businesses design their online interfaces is the right way to go. This would impose an unjustified burden on responsible traders, like us, without providing an effective and future-proof solution for consumers. Instead, a more effective enforcement of the current principle-based framework should be prioritized – and it could be achieved without changing the framework itself.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2. FLEXIBILITY FOR CONSUMER-CENTRIC INTERFACE DESIGN

evidence_quotes.quote: Our subscribers can choose to unsubscribe anytime
managing subscription cancellation

paragraphs.text: Paramount+ has a straightforward approach to managing subscription cancellation, in line with industry best practices. Our subscribers can choose to unsubscribe anytime via an intuitive user journey through their account settings, where we politely and respectfully ensure that there is nothing that we can do to incentivize them to remain.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.1. Consumers need to be aware of retention offers as part of the cancellation process

evidence_quotes.quote: an easy online cancellation method that is available to a subscriber from their account settings

paragraphs.text: We are generally supportive of placing a duty on traders to provide an easy online cancellation method that is available to a subscriber from their account settings, but do not believe that this necessarily needs to be a single ‘cancellation button’ as proposed in the consultation questionnaire. Rather, there needs to be flexibility for SVOD services on how to implement such a provision, to enable them to make offers that are in the consumer’s interest to know about.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.1. Consumers need to be aware of retention offers as part of the cancellation process

evidence_quotes.quote: they are very common across subscription services

paragraphs.text: We reject the assumption that such features and offers lead to anti-consumer outcomes. On the contrary, they are very common across subscription services and seen by consumers as a natural and welcome part of the commercial relationship, especially in such a competitive sector where consumers hold the upper hand. Offering consumers the opportunity to save money while still being able to enjoy AV content legally should be welcome rather than obstructed.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.1. Consumers need to be aware of retention offers as part of the cancellation process

evidence_quotes.quote: We are not in the business of tricking consumers into making unwanted choices.

paragraphs.text: Consumers come to our streaming services because of the great content catalogue on offer. We are not in the business of tricking consumers into making unwanted choices. On the contrary, consumer trust is key for businesses like ours that are based on recurrent interactions, not a one-off exchange. This is amplified by the fact that the SVOD market is extremely competitive, thus a bad customer experience could lead subscribers to leave our services in favor of competitors.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.1. Consumers need to be aware of retention offers as part of the cancellation process

evidence_quotes.quote: streaming subscription cancellations

paragraphs.text: A one-size-fits-all prescriptive approach to streaming subscription cancellations, without the ability to offer incentives to remain, will likely increase consumer churn unnecessarily and mean fewer resources available for investment in new content.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.1. Consumers need to be aware of retention offers as part of the cancellation process

evidence_quotes.quote: the so-called ‘autoplay’ feature
design features

paragraphs.text: After the end of an episode in a series, our subscribers can watch the next episode automatically – the so-called ‘autoplay’ feature. This provides them with a seamless user experience, comparable to the continuous broadcast stream of linear TV channels. In the context of editorial content produced respecting existing audience protection measures, this feature cannot be considered addictive in and of itself. It should not be confused with other types of design features, such as those enabling users to keep watching virtually limitless hours of user-generated content on video-sharing platforms. Furthermore, in line with industry best practices, we already give users the option to turn off ‘autoplay’ through an intuitive button that is easily reachable in their account settings.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.2. Autoplay and content recommendations are important features for an optimal VOD experience

evidence_quotes.quote: The consultation questionnaire also raises concerns around the potential addictiveness of certain recommender systems.
According to internal research, most of Paramount+ users expect and appreciate receiving personalized recommendations.

paragraphs.text: The consultation questionnaire also raises concerns around the potential addictiveness of certain recommender systems. Here, we would like to underscore the importance of taking into account the context of recommendations and the business model of the traders that use them. On streaming services, algorithmic recommendations are fundamental for consumers to discover AV content that is most relevant for them, as acknowledged by the European Media Industry Outlook 2025. According to internal research, most of Paramount+ users expect and appreciate receiving personalized recommendations. Moreover, this is one of the ways in which our services are editorially curated, and it should be understood as distinct from services that do not have editorial responsibility for their services or content.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.2. Autoplay and content recommendations are important features for an optimal VOD experience

evidence_quotes.quote: addictive design techniques
digital literacy and education campaigns

paragraphs.text: The issue of digital addiction is both incredibly important and complex. Introducing one-size-fits-all rules would create an overly prescriptive and imprecise framework that may fail to address these challenges effectively. Instead, it is crucial to adopt a risk-based approach that recognizes that ‘autoplay’ and content recommendation systems used by streamers are transparent, well-understood and valued by consumers, and are fundamentally different to addictive design techniques that might be seen as problematic. Finally, the key role of digital literacy and education campaigns for empowering parents, minors and consumers more broadly should not be underestimated, and such initiatives should be prioritized as opposed to proposing new prescriptive rules.

section_path: 1. A BALANCED APPROACH TO DIGITAL SUBSCRIPTIONS
2.2. Autoplay and content recommendations are important features for an optimal VOD experience

paragraphs.text: This chapter responds to Sections 4 and 5 of the public consultation.

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

paragraphs.text: Advertising has been an important revenue stream for media providers for many years. Laws and regulations have been developed over this time to protect consumers and are already sufficient to deal with concerns identified in the Commission’s consultation. The Commission should therefore:

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

evidence_quotes.quote: personalized advertising as problematic

paragraphs.text: Avoid labelling all personalized advertising as problematic and duplicating rules, as consumers are already protected by very robust guardrails.

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

paragraphs.text: Avoid introducing legally vague new concepts, such as ‘negative mental states’, that risk inadvertently restricting unproblematic forms of advertising.

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

evidence_quotes.quote: influencers adhere to practices that ensure the protection of consumer interests.

paragraphs.text: Look to existing rules, self-regulation and industry best practices to ensure influencers adhere to practices that ensure the protection of consumer interests.

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

paragraphs.text: Advertising is an important source of revenue for Europe’s audiovisual sector and supports investment in the production of new content. Streaming platforms, which have previously relied on subscription revenues, are looking to diversify revenues and price points through the use of advertising. Free, ad-supported streaming television (FAST) services, pioneered by Pluto TV, also provide an additional more economical option for consumers.

section_path: 3. ADVERTISING, INFLUENCERS, AND THE VALUE OF PERSONALIZATION

evidence_quotes.quote: The ability to target advertising towards individuals, or groups of individuals,
consumers often find personalized content and offers useful.

paragraphs.text: The ability to target advertising towards individuals, or groups of individuals, increases its efficacy in terms of relevance to the consumer as well as value to the advertiser. To this point, it is encouraging that the Commission itself acknowledges in its consultation questionnaire that consumers often find personalized content and offers useful. Paramount understands and respects the privacy and data protection concerns related to this issue but believes that consumers are already sufficiently protected by very robust guardrails under the current comprehensive framework composed of the GDPR, DSA, DMA, AVMSD and ePrivacy Directive.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers

evidence_quotes.quote: profiling and behaviorally targeted advertising

paragraphs.text: Notably, the GDPR strictly limits the instances in which special categories of personal data can be processed, including for advertising. Additionally, the AVMSD prohibits the processing of personal data of minors, which is collected or generated by media service providers, for commercial purposes, including profiling and behaviorally targeted advertising.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers

paragraphs.text: Paramount’s advertising practices are consistent with the above-mentioned EU laws and additional national protections in the Member States we operate in, as well as with stringent rules set by the U.S. Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act and the UK’s Age Appropriate Design Code.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers

paragraphs.text: New measures, especially for regulated services like ours, would amount to a duplication of existing obligations raising concerns over regulatory coherence and complexity. Should any further policy action be pursued, it must be proportionate and focus on the right level of the value chain.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers

evidence_quotes.quote: personalized ads that use certain types of information about consumers' vulnerability.
'negative mental states' of consumers as one of these information categories.

paragraphs.text: We understand that the Commission is considering a possible restriction of personalized ads that use certain types of information about consumers' vulnerability. In this context, the consultation refers to 'negative mental states' of consumers as one of these information categories. While we agree with the general objective of protecting consumers from unfair practices, we would like to point out that introducing vague concepts like 'negative mental states' would bring legal uncertainty and potentially discourage legitimate practices.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.2. Legitimate and non-intrusive advertising practices impacted by legally vague provisions

evidence_quotes.quote: limit our ability to offer relevant ads based on the audiovisual content consumers are watching

paragraphs.text: In fact, it could have the effect of restricting contextual advertising, which is less intrusive than other types of targeting. For example, it could limit our ability to offer relevant ads based on the audiovisual content consumers are watching (e.g. genre, tone, underlying emotional vibe). Therefore, we caution against the prohibition of personalized advertising based on such vague references.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.2. Legitimate and non-intrusive advertising practices impacted by legally vague provisions

evidence_quotes.quote: Advertising self-regulation has a significant role to play in ensuring responsible and transparent influencer marketing practices, as recognized by the Council conclusions of May 2024.

paragraphs.text: Advertising self-regulation has a significant role to play in ensuring responsible and transparent influencer marketing practices, as recognized by the Council conclusions of May 2024. The work of the European Advertising Standards Alliance (EASA) has been vital in driving transparency and trust in this space. Instead of proposing additional requirements on product-specific marketing, we encourage the Commission to take into consideration existing industry efforts, which are concrete steps to protect more vulnerable groups.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.3. Effective self-regulation and proportionality of responsibilities in influencer marketing are key

evidence_quotes.quote: Therefore, influencers should be held solely liable for ensuring transparency of their commercial communications and for the content they promote.

paragraphs.text: We believe that all actors in the influencer marketing ecosystem have a role to play in ensuring responsible practices. The level of responsibility should be proportionate to each actor's role in the value chain and the degree of control and visibility they have over the content. Therefore, influencers should be held solely liable for ensuring transparency of their commercial communications and for the content they promote.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.3. Effective self-regulation and proportionality of responsibilities in influencer marketing are key

evidence_quotes.quote: require influencers to clearly and conspicuously disclose any connections to Paramount

paragraphs.text: Paramount's approach to its relationship with influencers reflects these principles. Our contracts with influencers and their agencies require influencers to clearly and conspicuously disclose any connections to Paramount and to comply with all applicable regulations and laws – including, but not limited to, media, advertising, consumer protection, and minors' protection laws. They usually also include links to relevant guidance from national authorities so that influencers are aware of their responsibilities.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.3. Effective self-regulation and proportionality of responsibilities in influencer marketing are key

evidence_quotes.quote: obligations are being considered with regards to influencers whose followership is mainly composed of minors

paragraphs.text: We understand that certain obligations are being considered with regards to influencers whose followership is mainly composed of minors, and we fully support the protection of minors as a fundamental policy objective. It is also crucial to ensure legal clarity and proportionality. For example, introducing a blanket prohibition of the use of AI-generated or retouched images by influencers could limit creativity and artistic expression by restricting harmless use-cases. Moreover, the use of AI-generated images is already covered by transparency obligations under the AI Act.

section_path: 3.1. Existing laws on targeted advertising provide sufficient protection for consumers
3.3. Effective self-regulation and proportionality of responsibilities in influencer marketing are key

paragraphs.text: The contribution of the Central European Lawyers Initiative (CELI) is provided in the attached document.
